claims.claim_title: PCM2 reload semantics are represented explicitly
claims.summary: The lecture one-line workspace reload contract is reconstructed from governed repository artifacts instead of being left as an opaque external assumption.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 baseline GLS outputs match before phylogenetic correction
claims.summary: The non-phylogenetic regression slope, intercept, fit statistics, and coefficient decisions align before any phylogenetic covariance is introduced.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 Pagel-lambda regression agrees within governed tolerance
claims.summary: Estimated-lambda regression outputs keep the same analytical conclusion while allowing bounded numerical drift between R and Bijux implementations.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 phylogenetic signal testing agrees within governed tolerance
claims.summary: Lambda estimation and lambda-zero likelihood-ratio testing preserve the same signal conclusion while tolerating bounded numerical differences.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 residual diagnostics are machine-recorded and scientifically equivalent
claims.summary: Residual diagnostics remain reviewer-visible as governed scalar summaries instead of being trapped in hand-inspected plots.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 transformed-tree workflows match on governed branch summaries
claims.summary: OU, early-burst, and late-burst rescaling stay reviewable through deterministic branch and total-length comparisons before downstream comparative claims are made.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 fitContinuous-style evolutionary mode fits agree within governed tolerance
claims.summary: Brownian, OU, and early-burst intercept fits preserve the same parameter and model-fit story while allowing bounded numerical drift from the R reference.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 likelihood-ratio model comparisons agree within governed tolerance
claims.summary: BM-versus-OU, BM-versus-early-burst, and OU-versus-early-burst test statistics stay aligned under explicit tolerance rules.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 Brownian and early-burst ancestral reconstructions agree within governed tolerance
claims.summary: Internal-node ancestral estimates stay comparable across the canonical runtime and governed R reference on named node identities.

claims.claim_title: PCM2 remaining intercept-mode likelihood sweep boundary stays explicit
claims.summary: The lecture corBlomberg likelihood sweep is kept visible as a bounded trust surface instead of being silently implied by the new parity bundles.
